body:
## What changed

- Made `validate-content` route supported `--focus-*` modes through `scripts/validate-content-focus-registry.js` IDs instead of raw `process.argv.includes(...)` checks.
- Limited the registry to currently implemented focused validators, so unsupported focus flags fail fast before loading TypeScript/project modules.
- Added a focused summary-key guard so each focused validator emits exactly the keys registered for that route.
- Added routing/meta coverage in `tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js` for registry parity, isolated summaries, and unknown focus rejection.

## Why

Focus modes were split between ad hoc CLI checks and a registry that also advertised modes without validator branches. That allowed unsupported or unfinished focus flags to fall through to full validation and made summary-key drift easy to miss.

## Validation

- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" node --test tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js`
- all registered focus flags executed once with `node scripts/validate-content.js <flag>`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run validate:content`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run lint`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run test:ownership`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run format:check -- scripts/validate-content.js scripts/validate-content-focus-registry.js tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js`
- `node --check scripts/validate-content.js && node --check scripts/validate-content-focus-registry.js && node --check tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js`
- `git diff --check`
title: [codex] Centralize validate-content focus routes
